title: Identity-ceremony authoring — what a passkey/FedCM enrollment Configurator domain would expose
summary: Grounds decision #499 (identity-ceremony Configurator domain). The credential-acquisition build (#483/#496, resolved) deliberately OMITS a Configurator domain — technical defaults ride the CustomCredentialProvider registry + config-flavors. #499 asks whether, on a real authoring need, an identity domain should be added to the plateau-app Technical Configurator (data-driven: seed + provider entry, like the webvalidation/file-upload domain #725) and what it would expose. The #499 review classified this seam as RESEARCH-GATED — not priority:low (a guessed enrollment-config default would be wrong) and not a maturityGated park (it waits on a prior-art survey we can do now, not on adoption). SCOPE: this subject is AUTHORING (how a non-coder configures an enrollment ceremony), distinct from the credential-acquisition PROTOCOL (we:src/_data/researchTopics/credential-acquisition-protocol.json) and the webidentity PROJECT (we:src/_data/researchTopics/web-identity-project.json), which are their own subjects. PRIOR ART TO SURVEY: (1) Web platform (authoritative) — WebAuthn/passkeys registration ceremony (navigator.credentials.create; PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptions: RP, user, authenticatorSelection, attestation, residentKey/discoverable-credential, userVerification) + FedCM (navigator.credentials.get + IdP config endpoint); these define the author-facing AXES. (2) Low-code identity tooling (benchmark) — the SHAPE hosted identity platforms use to expose enrollment-flow config to non-coders (knob-form vs visual flow vs policy matrix). (3) In-repo — the plateau-app Technical Configurator data-driven domain pattern (seed + provider entry) shipped for webvalidation/file-upload (#725), the candidate home. SURVEY EXECUTED 2026-06-23 (results now in the report, all four open questions answered). Q1 author-facing axes: a SMALL policy-shaped set — userVerification (required/preferred/discouraged), residentKey/discoverable, authenticatorAttachment (platform/cross-platform), attestation (enterprise tier only), FedCM mediation (optional/required/silent), timeout; everything else is BRANDING (rp.name/icon — wrong tool) or IMPL-ONLY (challenge/user.id/pubKeyCredParams/excludeCredentials — the provider's job). Q2: POLICY surface (independent enumerated knobs, no ordered steps) NOT a flow surface — maps onto the plateau Configurator Axis model. Q3: CLEAN instance of the existing data-driven domain pattern, ZERO new mechanism — one seed module (plateau:src/technical-configurator/seed-<id>.ts, twin plateau:src/technical-configurator/seed-file-upload.ts:265-271) + one DOMAINS provider entry (plateau:src/technical-configurator/provider.ts:26-48); credential families = strategies. Q4 (the demand gate): NO authoring consumer exists today — WE's only identity surface (we:demos/webidentity-conformance-demo.ts) hardcodes its own DemoCredentialProvider; the plateau Configurator's ~17 domains include none for identity; plateau:src/dev-browser/credential-source is git-forge write-auth (#600), a different problem. Benchmark: Clerk/Auth0 expose passkey policy as dashboard TOGGLES (= the Axis knob-matrix); Descope's visual Flow editor solves multi-step orchestration, a DIFFERENT surface. RECOMMENDED DEFAULT (to ratify in #499): (b) HOLD on a concrete-consumer trigger — do NOT build now (a Configurator domain no author can reach, duplicating axes the CustomCredentialProvider registry + config-flavors already carry), do NOT permanently dissolve (survey proves it is now cheaply buildable: ~1-day seed task). Un-park trigger: first flagship/exercise-app flow needing an author to tune passkey/FedCM enrollment WITHOUT writing a plug. Confidence HIGH (shape + no-consumer both unambiguous). Full survey + sources in we:reports/2026-06-22-identity-ceremony-authoring.md.
